# UC-23.3.5 · Paid Media Cost Per Acquisition and Quality Score

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Business &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch paid placements so pricey auctions cannot silently drain budgets before finance notices shrinking returns. We compare invoice-grade spend against measured conversions and auction-quality grades so teams rebalance bids before quarters slip.*

---

## Description

Monitors normalized paid-placement feeds plus CRM-attributed outcomes so performance marketers see blended acquisition cost, seller-grade relevance signals (Quality Score bands on auction search lanes, Quality Impact deltas on Microsoft Advertising, relevance stacks where walled gardens expose rankings instead of QS integers), ROAS/MER cousins, pacing stress, and impression-share loss split across rank-lost versus budget-lost versus eligibility-lost slices. Splunk stitches OAuth-polled channel facts with Salesforce closed-won bridges and optional first-touch UTM governance rows so Splunk-native thresholds highlight programmes spending heavily while auctions degrade—distinct from blended portfolio ROI studies (UC-23.3.1), funnel-stage leakage scans (UC-23.3.2), inbox-deliverability telemetry (UC-23.3.3), SEO/traffic sourcing from raw server logs (UC-23.3.4), content lift experiments (UC-23.3.6), and webinar attribution packs (UC-23.3.7). Cross-check narrative overlaps with UC-23.1.5 only where harmonized attribution windows feed CPA comparisons.

## Value

Chief marketing officers and performance-media directors negotiate reallocations faster when Splunk aligns invoice-ready USD spend with CRM-backed acquisition economics instead of trusting isolated auction consoles—protecting quarterly pipeline commitments before treasury closes books. FP&A pairs pacing_health tiles with CFO-ready MER versus Magic Number overlays when benchmarking SaaS portfolios against Bessemer Cloud Index quartiles (Magic Number north of ~0.85 for top cohorts). Demand-gen operators compare CPA deltas versus WordStream-class CTR baselines while guarding budgets against QS erosion that lifts CPC fifty percent despite unchanged bids.

## Implementation

Provision OAuth modular inputs plus Splunk credential objects per walled garden; normalize GAQL JSON (microcurrency fields ÷ 1,000,000), Insights JSON (`cost_per_action_type` arrays flattened upstream), LinkedIn UTC timestamps, TikTok bidding enums; maintain KV lookups `fx_rates_daily.csv`, `customer_ltv_by_segment.csv`, `utm_campaign_taxonomy.csv`, `target_cpa_thresholds.csv`; mirror Salesforce Opportunities via Splunk DB Connect or Splunk Add-on for Salesforce; terminate Google Tag Manager / GA4 tagging proofs through HEC for UTM audits; publish Dashboard Studio workbook `paid_media_qs_roas` fed by saved search above.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Heavy Forwarder cluster sized for HTTPS egress toward Google Ads (`googleads.googleapis.com`), Meta Graph (`graph.facebook.com`), LinkedIn (`api.linkedin.com`), TikTok Business API hosts; rotated OAuth secrets stored under Splunk storage passwords.
- Developer artifacts — Google Ads manager-linked developer token, OAuth web/app credentials with offline refresh tokens; Meta System User tokens plus Marketing API version pinned (`v20.0` sample); LinkedIn Developer Application client credentials with `r_ads_reporting` scopes; Microsoft Advertising developer token paired with Bing Ads OAuth.
- Splunk indexes (`business`, optional `marketing_intel`) permitted on analyst roles; KV Store collections (`paid_media_checkpoint`) storing GAQL `next_page_token`, Meta `after` cursor, LinkedIn `paging.start` offsets.
- CRM hygiene workbook tying Salesforce Campaign IDs (`701…`) or HubSpot campaign tokens to downstream feeds — mismatched bridges inflate orphan spend rows.

### Step 1 — Configure onboarding lanes
Google Ads Path — Craft modular input invoking GAQL strings such as `SELECT campaign.id, campaign.name, metrics.cost_micros, metrics.impressions, metrics.clicks, metrics.conversions, metrics.search_impression_share, metrics.search_rank_lost_impression_share, metrics.search_budget_lost_impression_share, metrics.quality_score, metrics.ctr, metrics.average_cpc FROM campaign WHERE segments.date DURING LAST_30_DAYS`. Respect microcurrency division (`cost_micros / 1000000`). Rotate developer-token approvals annually.
Microsoft Advertising Path — Schedule Bulk Service downloads plus Reporting SOAP/REST pulls surfacing Quality Impact alongside CPC.
Meta Path — Poll Insights (`fields=campaign_name,spend,impressions,clicks,actions,conversion_values,cost_per_action_type`) with attribution_setting diagnostics mirrored into Splunk (`attribution_setting`, `action_report_time`). Pair browser Pixel deliveries with Conversions API hashed payloads (`event_id`, `event_name`) so AEM duplicates collapse.
LinkedIn Path — Issue REST `/rest/adAnalytics` queries (`dateRange=(start,end)`, `pivot=CAMPAIGN`) with projections requesting `costInLocalCurrency`, `impressions`, `clicks`, `oneClickLeads`, `leadGenerationMailContactInfoShares`; normalize `_time` using UTC markers supplied by LinkedIn JSON.
TikTok Path — Authenticate OAuth with Marketing API (`/oauth2/access_token/`), pull `/report/integrated/get/` structures enumerating bidding modes (`BID_TYPE_NO_BID`, cost-cap aliases).
Aggregator Path — Accept Supermetrics/Funnel CSV drops via monitored directory ingestion only after vendor QA worksheets verify parity versus native consoles.
CRM Proof Path — Configure Splunk DB Connect JDBC warehouse pulls (`SELECT campaign_key, stage_name, amount, campaign_bridge FROM mart.opportunity_facts`) or Splunk Add-on for Salesforce incremental Opportunity replication feeding `salesforce:opportunity`.
First-party tagging Path — Mirror Google Tag Manager container revisions via syslog or HEC so UTMs remain auditable alongside Enhanced Conversions hashed identifiers.
Props skeleton:
[paid_media:google_ads_gaql]
KV_MODE = json
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
TIME_PREFIX = \",
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
Sample vendor payload excerpts embedded for onboarding QA:
{"results":[{"campaign":{"id":"123","name":"Brand_US"},"metrics":{"costMicros":"45230000","impressions":"82340","clicks":"5120","conversions":140.5,"searchImpressionShare":0.43,"qualityScore":8,"averageCpc":"8830"}}]}
{"data":[{"campaign_name":"Q3_2024_LandingPage","spend":"4523.00","impressions":823400,"clicks":51200,"actions":[{"action_type":"offsite_conversion.fb_pixel_purchase","value":"140"}],"cost_per_action_type":[{"action_type":"offsite_conversion.fb_pixel_purchase","value":"32.31"}]}]}
LinkedIn stylized snippet:
{"elements":[{"costInLocalCurrency":4523.15,"impressions":823400,"clicks":51200,"oneClickLeads":140,"leadGenerationMailContactInfoShares":32}]}
Currency normalization — multiply native amounts by lookup `fx_rates_daily.csv` keyed on (`report_date`,`currency_code`). Timezone normalization — store vendor-supplied account timezone strings beside each poll (`tz_anchor`) while Splunk `_time` stays UTC unless Heavy Forwarders rewrite ingestion clocks using scripted offsets documented per vendor.
Schema mapping canonical columns — `platform`, `account_id`, `campaign_id`, `campaign_name`, `adset_id`, `ad_id`, `spend_usd`, `impressions`, `clicks`, `reported_conversions`, `reported_revenue_usd`, `quality_score_or_relevance`, `bid_strategy`, `creative_format`.
Optional nightly audit compares Splunk aggregates versus DV360 Query Tool exports when programmatic CSV feeds reconcile slowly.

### Step 2 — Encode SPL macros plus lookups
Persist normalization macros (`normalize_platform`, `normalize_currency`) inside `macros.conf`. Maintain KV lookups documented above plus `salesforce_opportunities.csv` trimmed nightly from Salesforce Bulk extracts (`lead_id`, `closed_won_value`, `days_to_close`) for companion lead-grain searches—apply `lookup salesforce_opportunities.csv lead_id OUTPUT closed_won_value days_to_close` before rolling leads into campaign dashboards when sourcetype=`sfdc:lead` snapshots supply identifiers.
For UC-23.8.5 Magic Number overlays, reuse FP&A-provided spreadsheet macros rather than recomputing inside this SPL.
Saved search cadence — hourly enrichment micro-pass on pacing tiles; rolling thirty-day CPA alert nightly.
Append supplemental correlation panels sourcing UC-23.1.5 attribution spine only after agreeing harmonized seven-day click windows before comparing Meta versus Google CPA headline ratios.

### Step 3 — Validate numerically
Compare Splunk outputs row-by-row against Google Ads Reports Editor totals (`metrics.cost_micros` sums), Microsoft Advertising Reports UI, Meta Ads Manager Insights CSV exports (matching attribution windows), LinkedIn Campaign Manager downloadable spreadsheets, TikTok Ads Manager summaries.
Cross-read GA4 Acquisition reports (`Traffic acquisition`) ensuring UTMs align with Splunk `utm_campaign_taxonomy.csv` aliases.
Salesforce validation — export Opportunity dashboards filtered by Campaign Influence equals Splunk bridge keys; reconcile closed-won counts versus Splunk `won_opportunities`.
Instrument QA worksheets documenting tolerance bands — ±2 percent acceptable on spend when aggregator rounding differs.

### Step 4 — Operationalize alerting plus cadence
Slack or Microsoft Teams webhook routing performance-media pods whenever `channel_cpa_blended` breaches `target_cpa_threshold`, `cpa_delta_pct` exceeds twenty-five percent week-over-week, or `ltv_adjusted_roas` slips below `min_roas_floor` (~1.5 illustrative).
Executive ROI digest weekly summarizing pacing_health anomalies (`Over-Pace` > fifteen percent drift versus linear expectation).
Budget pacing alarm mid-month when `budget_used_pct` minus linear expectation surpasses fifteen positive or negative points.
Embed Splunk Phantom or SOAR optional enrichments referencing Auction Insights CSV uploads when competitor spike hypotheses arise.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting modes
1. Mobile ATT sampling stays beneath twenty-five percent — Meta relies on Aggregated Event Measurement modeling so deterministic conversions crater thirty-to-sixty percent versus pre-April 2021 baselines despite steady demand; compare modeled versus verified cohort columns inside Ads Manager before blaming bids.
2. Attribution-window mismatch — Meta defaults to one-day click plus seven-day click mixes post-iOS 14.5 while Google Ads frequently reports thirty-day click; harmonise exported CSV using identical seven-day click slices before declaring CPA winners.
3. QS placeholders — nascent keywords display Quality Score `--` until roughly one hundred impressions accrue; sudden jumps simply reflect cohort eligibility finishing rather than creative miracles.
4. Smart Bidding learning gates — campaigns lacking fifty weekly conversions oscillate wildly; suppress SLA thresholds until exit signals confirm stability.
5. View-through conversions bundled inside totals inflate ROAS — exclude VTC slices inside offline spreadsheets mirrored back into Splunk macros when evaluating DR programmes.
6. Cross-device modeled lifts — Google Ads adds eight-to-fifteen percent modeled conversions while LinkedIn lacks parity; annotate dashboards calling comparisons unfair unless modeled toggles align.
7. Reporting-date skew — auctions attribute conversions to click timestamps while finance recognises revenue by booking date — September clicks plus October conversions distort MTD SPL joins unless treasury overlays accepted.
8. Brand versus generic QS blending — branded programmes skew QS nine-to-ten masking generic deterioration; isolate portfolio splits via supplemental WHERE clauses.
9. Pixel suppression plus duplicate server events — Brave and Safari ITP mute browser fires until Conversions API duplicates arrive; dedupe strictly on `event_id` hashes.
10. Daily budget exhaustion midday stops auctions — CPA spikes trace pacing starvation rather than relevance decay.
11. Competitor entrants spike CPC forty percent inside Auction Insights share columns — CPA climbs absent creative regressions.
12. Peak-season CPC inflation — Cyber-five spans elevate CPC multiples yet conversion rates also spike; insist YoY cohort comparisons rather than WoW deltas alone.
13. Checkout-page instrumentation leaks — QA revisits bookmarked `/thank-you` URLs inflate conversions.
14. Privacy Sandbox transitions — Topics API plus Attribution Reporting summaries gradually replace third-party pixels; Splunk ingest requires CRM-heavy joins sooner.
15. Structural campaign splits reset histories — trending macros must pivot on immutable objective tags rather than transient IDs.
16. Strategy swaps — migrating between target CPA and target ROAS resets learning phases producing fourteen-day instability unless alerts mute transitions.
17. Sophisticated invalid traffic inflates impressions without conversions — CTR collapses resembling fatigue though auction telemetry stays healthy when MOAT or DoubleVerify overlays absent.
18. Dynamic Creative Optimization mixes creatives within single auctions — CPA attribution stays nondeterministic unless creative asset IDs populate granular feeds.

## SPL

```spl
index=business (sourcetype="paid_media:google_ads_gaql" OR sourcetype="paid_media:microsoft_reporting" OR sourcetype="paid_media:meta_insights" OR sourcetype="paid_media:linkedin_analytics" OR sourcetype="paid_media:tiktok_reports" OR sourcetype="paid_media:social_bulk" OR sourcetype="paid_media:dsp_spend") earliest=-30d@d latest=now
| eval report_date=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval platform_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(platform,vendor,ad_network,data_source,"")))
| eval platform=case(match(platform_raw,"google|ads_search|dsa"),"google_ads", match(platform_raw,"bing|microsoft|msn"),"microsoft_ads", match(platform_raw,"meta|facebook|instagram"),"meta", match(platform_raw,"linkedin"),"linkedin", match(platform_raw,"tiktok"),"tiktok", match(platform_raw,"reddit"),"reddit", match(platform_raw,"pinterest"),"pinterest", match(platform_raw,"snap"),"snapchat", match(platform_raw,"twitter|\\bx\\b"),"twitter_ads", match(platform_raw,"ttd|dv360|stackadapt|criteo|dsp|amazon\\sdsp"),"dsp", 1=1,"other")
| eval account_id=coalesce(customer_id,account_id,ad_account_id)
| eval campaign_id=coalesce('campaign.id',campaign_id,campaignId)
| eval campaign_name=coalesce('campaign.name',campaign_name,campaign_name_std,campaign_name_alt)
| eval campaign_slug=lower(trim(coalesce(campaign_slug,utm_campaign,campaign_external_key,"")))
| eval adset_id=coalesce(ad_group_id,adset_id,ad_group.id)
| eval ad_id=coalesce(ad_id,ad.id,creative_id,creative.id)
| eval currency_code=upper(trim(coalesce(account_currency,currency,currency_code,"USD")))
| eval spend_micros=tonumber(coalesce(cost_micros,costMicros,metrics.cost_micros,"0"))
| eval spend_native=case(platform IN ("google_ads","microsoft_ads") AND spend_micros>0, spend_micros/1000000.0, platform="meta", tonumber(coalesce(spend,spend_usd,"0")), platform="linkedin", tonumber(coalesce(costInLocalCurrency,spend,"0")), 1=1, tonumber(coalesce(spend,spend_usd,amount_local,"0")))
| lookup fx_rates_daily date AS report_date currency_code OUTPUT rate_to_usd AS fx_rate
| eval spend_usd=if(isnotnull(fx_rate), round(spend_native*fx_rate,4), spend_native)
| eval impressions=tonumber(coalesce(impressions,metrics.impressions,"0"))
| eval clicks=tonumber(coalesce(clicks,metrics.clicks,"0"))
| eval reported_conversions=tonumber(coalesce(metrics.conversions,conversions,purchase_actions,"0"))
| eval reported_revenue_usd=tonumber(coalesce(conversion_values,revenue_usd,purchase_value,"0"))
| eval quality_score_or_relevance=tonumber(coalesce(metrics.quality_score,quality_score,qualityImpact,""))
| eval qs_numeric=if(isnum(quality_score_or_relevance),quality_score_or_relevance,null())
| eval average_cpc_micros=tonumber(coalesce(metrics.average_cpc,average_cpc_micros,"0"))
| eval average_cpc_usd=case(platform IN ("google_ads","microsoft_ads") AND average_cpc_micros>1000, average_cpc_micros/1000000.0, average_cpc_micros>0 AND average_cpc_micros<500, average_cpc_micros, 1=1, tonumber(average_cpc_micros))
| eval bid_strategy=upper(trim(coalesce(bidding_strategy,bidding_strategy_type,bidding_strategy_enum,"UNKNOWN")))
| eval bid_strategy_bucket=case(bid_strategy="MANUAL_CPC","manual", bid_strategy IN ("MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS","TARGET_CPA","TARGET_ROAS","MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE"),"smart_bidding", match(bid_strategy,"MAXIMIZE"),"smart_bidding", 1=1,"other")
| eval creative_format=coalesce(creative_format,ad_format,placement,"unknown")
| eval rank_lost_share=tonumber(coalesce(metrics.search_rank_lost_impression_share,search_rank_lost_impression_share,"0"))
| eval budget_lost_share=tonumber(coalesce(metrics.search_budget_lost_impression_share,search_budget_lost_impression_share,"0"))
| eval eligibility_lost_share=tonumber(coalesce(metrics.search_exact_match_impression_share_lost_eligibility,exact_match_eligibility_lost,"0"))
| eval quality_score_band=case(qs_numeric>=8,"Above-Avg", qs_numeric IN (5,6,7),"Average", isnotnull(qs_numeric),"Below-Avg", 1=1,"unknown")
| eval tz_anchor=coalesce(account_timezone,"America/New_York")
| eval reporting_stamp=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eventstats avg(average_cpc_usd) AS qc_avg_cpc_band by quality_score_band platform
| stats sum(spend_usd) AS spend_usd sum(impressions) AS impressions sum(clicks) AS clicks sum(reported_conversions) AS reported_conversions sum(reported_revenue_usd) AS reported_revenue_usd avg(qs_numeric) AS avg_quality_score avg(average_cpc_usd) AS qc_avg_cpc avg(rank_lost_share) AS avg_rank_lost avg(budget_lost_share) AS avg_budget_lost avg(eligibility_lost_share) AS avg_eligibility_lost dc(ad_id) AS creative_variants values(bid_strategy_bucket) AS bid_buckets max(qc_avg_cpc_band) AS qc_band_peer_avg first(campaign_slug) AS campaign_slug latest(reporting_stamp) AS last_reporting_anchor by platform account_id campaign_id campaign_name quality_score_band
| eval channel_cpa_blended=if(reported_conversions>0, round(spend_usd/reported_conversions,2), null())
| lookup utm_campaign_taxonomy.csv campaign_slug OUTPUT qualified_lead_definition cadence_notes
| join type=left campaign_id max=50000 [
    search index=business (sourcetype="marketo:activities" OR sourcetype="hubspot:lead_touch" OR sourcetype="crm:mql_bridge") earliest=-30d@d latest=now
    | eval campaign_key=lower(trim(coalesce(utm_campaign,campaign_external_id,"")))
    | stats dc(lead_hash) AS qualified_leads sum(eval(if(match(lower(status_bucket),"qualified|accepted"),1,0))) AS gov_touch_volume by campaign_key
    | rename campaign_key AS campaign_id
]
| fillnull value=0 qualified_leads
| eval channel_cpa_qualified=if(qualified_leads>0, round(spend_usd/qualified_leads,2), null())
| join type=left campaign_id max=50000 [
    search index=business (sourcetype="salesforce:opportunity" OR sourcetype="dbx:crm_opportunities") earliest=-365d@d latest=now
    | eval bridge_key=lower(trim(coalesce(CampaignId,Campaign_Key,campaign_bridge,"")))
    | eval IsWon_flag=if(like(lower(tostring(coalesce(IsWon,is_won,""))),"1") OR match(lower(StageName),"closed\\s?won"),1,0)
    | stats sum(eval(if(IsWon_flag=1,tonumber(coalesce(Amount,amount,"0")),0))) AS closed_won_value dc(Id) AS won_opportunities by bridge_key
    | rename bridge_key AS campaign_id
]
| fillnull value=0 won_opportunities
| eval cost_per_won_opp=if(isnotnull(won_opportunities) AND won_opportunities>0, round(spend_usd/won_opportunities,2), null())
| eval channel_roas=if(spend_usd>0 AND reported_revenue_usd>0, round(reported_revenue_usd/spend_usd,2), null())
| eval mer_ratio=if(spend_usd>0 AND reported_revenue_usd>0, round(reported_revenue_usd/spend_usd,4), null())
| eval segment=coalesce(customer_segment,company_segment,"general")
| lookup customer_ltv_by_segment.csv segment OUTPUT mo12_ltv_usd AS twelve_month_ltv
| eval ltv_adjusted_roas=if(spend_usd>0 AND isnotnull(twelve_month_ltv), round((reported_conversions*twelve_month_ltv)/spend_usd,2), null())
| lookup target_cpa_thresholds.csv platform OUTPUT target_cpa_threshold min_roas_floor
| fillnull value=999999 target_cpa_threshold
| fillnull value=1.5 min_roas_floor
| eval monthly_budget_usd=tonumber(coalesce(monthly_budget_usd,campaign_budget_usd,"250000"))
| eval spend_to_date_usd=spend_usd
| eval budget_used_pct=if(monthly_budget_usd>0, round(100*spend_to_date_usd/monthly_budget_usd,1), null())
| eval days_into_month=tonumber(strftime(now(),"%e"))
| eval pacing_health=case(days_into_month>0 AND budget_used_pct/nullif(days_into_month,0)*30 > 110,"Over-Pace", days_into_month>0 AND budget_used_pct/nullif(days_into_month,0)*30 < 90,"Under-Pace", 1=1,"On-Pace")
| bin _time span=7d
| sort campaign_id _time
| streamstats current=f window=1 last(channel_cpa_blended) AS previous_week_cpa by campaign_id
| eval cpa_delta_pct=if(isnotnull(previous_week_cpa) AND previous_week_cpa>0, round(100*(channel_cpa_blended-previous_week_cpa)/previous_week_cpa,1), null())
| eval spend_at_risk=if(channel_cpa_blended>target_cpa_threshold OR cpa_delta_pct>25 OR ltv_adjusted_roas<min_roas_floor OR (quality_score_band="Below-Avg" AND spend_usd>5000), spend_usd, 0)
| where channel_cpa_blended>target_cpa_threshold OR cpa_delta_pct>25 OR ltv_adjusted_roas<min_roas_floor OR (quality_score_band="Below-Avg" AND spend_usd>5000)
| sort - spend_at_risk
| table platform campaign_name campaign_id spend_usd impressions clicks reported_conversions channel_cpa_blended channel_cpa_qualified cost_per_won_opp channel_roas mer_ratio ltv_adjusted_roas quality_score_band avg_quality_score qc_avg_cpc avg_rank_lost avg_budget_lost pacing_health cpa_delta_pct bid_buckets spend_at_risk tz_anchor qc_band_peer_avg last_reporting_anchor
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio layout: splunk.timeline on spend_usd versus pacing_health annotations; splunk.scatter with X axis avg_quality_score or qc_avg_cpc, Y axis channel_cpa_blended, point size spend_usd, split by platform; splunk.boxplot summarizing CPA distribution across bid_strategy_bucket; splunk.singlevalue tiles for blended CPA week-over-week delta; splunk.table ranked by spend_at_risk including avg_rank_lost versus avg_budget_lost columns for diagnostics.

## Known False Positives

Treasury closes invoices using quarter-end FX prints while Splunk applies daily treasury curves — CPA ladders spike although bids never shifted until FX harmonisation reruns overnight. Concurrent UTC-boundary pulls from LinkedIn plus account-local snapshots from auction consoles duplicate conversions near midnight—CPA dips abruptly though budgets stayed steady until dedupe macros collapse overlapping pulls.
